caveats: Public showcase only. No real patient data, secure AHS data, private Snowflake data, or private AHS endpoints are used.
Internal ED operating state, model outputs, scenario effects, and unit-level operating values are synthetic demonstration values.
Public facts are used only where cited. Unit-level values without public evidence are labelled synthetic planning assumptions.

facts.value: The Stollery pediatric emergency department is described by AHS as the only specialized ED and referral centre for children in central and northern Alberta.
facts.source_title: AHS Emergency Department, Pediatric service listing
facts.showcase_implication: The public demo is Stollery-only and pediatric-referral oriented.

facts.source_title: AHS Emergency Department, Pediatric service listing
facts.showcase_implication: Open-data context focuses on Edmonton weather, access, AQHI, and Edmonton Zone ED signals.

facts.source_title: AHS Emergency Department, Pediatric service listing
facts.showcase_implication: Forecasts use continuous hourly demand and staffing assumptions.

facts.value: Children from birth up to their 18th birthday.
facts.source_title: AHS Emergency Department, Pediatric service listing
facts.showcase_implication: Synthetic age bands use newborn, neonate, infant, toddler, child, and adolescent cohorts.

facts.value: AHS estimated ED wait times represent time from triage assessment to physician assessment, not total hospital time.
facts.source_title: AHS estimated emergency department wait times
facts.showcase_implication: The public wait-time signal is shown as a front-door physician-wait proxy, not ED LOS.

facts.value: The Stollery ED page says the first person families meet is a triage nurse, followed by registration and either a bed or the waiting room.
facts.showcase_implication: The synthetic flow board starts with triage, registration, waiting, rooming, and physician assessment.

facts.value: A public Stollery Foundation article described the Stollery as operating 236 beds across sites in 2021 planning material.
facts.source_title: Stollery Foundation stand-alone children's hospital article
facts.showcase_implication: The unit grid uses 236 as public total-capacity context, while many service splits remain synthetic assumptions.

facts.value: The same public article listed 161 beds at Walter C. Mackenzie Health Sciences Centre, 69 neonatal intensive care beds at the Royal Alexandra site, and 6 intensive care beds at the Sturgeon Community Hospital NICU.
facts.source_title: Stollery Foundation stand-alone children's hospital article
facts.showcase_implication: The synthetic bed grid avoids claiming unsupported exact unit splits beyond this broad public context.

facts.value: The Hiller PICU public article described 16 single-family rooms and noted a previous 12-bed open unit with four beds added in 2017.
facts.showcase_implication: PICU capacity is represented as a public-fact anchor while operating occupancy remains synthetic.

facts.value: AHS publishes a weekly Edmonton ED length-of-stay summary that includes Stollery Children's Hospital.
facts.source_title: AHS Weekly Emergency Department Length of Stay Summary - Edmonton
facts.showcase_implication: Historical public performance context is represented as an open signal, not secure visit-level truth.

facts.value: Alberta publishes an aggregate respiratory virus dashboard covering viruses such as RSV, influenza, COVID-19, hMPV, parainfluenza, rhinovirus/enterovirus and others.
facts.source_title: Government of Alberta respiratory virus dashboard
facts.showcase_implication: Respiratory pressure is a public driver blended with synthetic pediatric ED history.

facts.value: Environment Canada publishes Edmonton Air Quality Health Index observations and forecasts.
facts.showcase_implication: Smoke and air-quality stress are represented as pediatric respiratory demand modifiers.

facts.value: 511 Alberta provides road conditions, events, alerts, weather stations, and related API documentation.
facts.showcase_implication: Travel/access friction is treated as a proxy for arrival timing and EMS offload variation, not an EMS feed.

assumptions.value: Specific current staffed-bed counts by pediatric service are not asserted as public facts. They are rounded synthetic planning values constrained to public total-capacity context.
Current queues, wait times, boarders, resource utilization, and bottleneck calls are synthetic and regenerated deterministically from the export seed.
Forecasts and scenario effects are synthetic model demonstrations intended to show product behavior, not operational truth.
